name: Seth Rich Murder
description: The Seth Rich murder refers to the unsolved killing of Seth Rich, a Democratic National Committee (DNC) staff member, in Washington, D.C., on July 10, 2016. Conspiracy theories surrounding his death posit that Rich was the source of leaked DNC emails provided to WikiLeaks during the 2016 presidential election, and that his murder was orchestrated as a result of this alleged involvement. These theories gained traction online and were amplified by some media outlets and political figures. Proponents often cite circumstantial evidence, such as the timing of Rich's death close to the DNC email leaks, and unsubstantiated claims of communication between Rich and WikiLeaks. They also point to perceived inconsistencies in the police investigation. However, law enforcement authorities maintain that Rich was the victim of a robbery gone wrong, citing evidence of other robberies in the area and the fact that Rich still had his wallet, phone, and watch when found. Mainstream media and fact-checking organizations have debunked the conspiracy theories, highlighting the lack of credible evidence and the potential harm caused by spreading misinformation. The theories have been criticized for exploiting Rich's death for political purposes and for inflicting further pain on his family. Investigations by news organizations and law enforcement have found no evidence to support the claim that Rich leaked DNC emails or that his murder was politically motivated. The conspiracy theories persist despite repeated debunking and continue to be a source of controversy.
subtitle: Unsolved murder and conspiracy theories.

claims.claim: Seth Rich leaked DNC emails to WikiLeaks.
claims.evidence: Anonymous sources claiming Rich's involvement; perceived timing of Rich's death with the email leaks.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence links Rich to the leaks; WikiLeaks has never confirmed Rich as a source; forensic analysis points to Russian hacking.

claims.claim: Rich's murder was a professional hit orchestrated to silence him.
claims.evidence: Speculation about the circumstances of the murder; perceived lack of motive for robbery.
claims.criticism: Police investigation indicates a robbery gone wrong; no evidence of a professional hit; Rich's valuables were not all taken.

claims.claim: The police investigation was deliberately mishandled to cover up the truth.
claims.evidence: Criticism of police procedures; perceived inconsistencies in the official narrative.
claims.criticism: The investigation followed standard procedures; no evidence of a cover-up has been found; alternative explanations for perceived inconsistencies exist.

keyFigures.name: Jack Burkman
keyFigures.role: Private investigator who promoted conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: Sean Hannity
keyFigures.role: Fox News host who amplified the theories.

keyFigures.name: Rod Wheeler
keyFigures.role: Private investigator who made unsubstantiated claims about the case.

keyFigures.name: Aaron Rich
keyFigures.role: Seth Rich's brother, who has actively debunked the conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: FactCheck.org
keyFigures.role: Organization that has debunked the conspiracy theories.

timeline.event: Seth Rich is murdered in Washington, D.C.
Conspiracy theories about Rich's death begin to emerge online.
Fox News retracts a story promoting the conspiracy theory.
Conspiracy theories continue to circulate despite debunking.
